page: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor, utilizing the Neoverse-V1 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, with a 1:1 mapping of vCPUs to physical cores and no hyperthreading. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Local storage is not bundled, and network performance is rated at an 8 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths placing in the top 25% at 186.18 GB/sec and 93.97 GB/sec respectively. CPU performance is generally average, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores falling in the middle 50%. Cryptographic operations show some limitations, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25%. LLM inference capabilities are moderate, achieving 96.74 tokens/sec for text generation on a small 135M model, but dropping to a weaker 28.19 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

The c7g.4xlarge offers a highly resource-dense profile for compute-intensive workloads. By utilizing dedicated ARM cores and high-bandwidth memory, it delivers efficient processing without the overhead of hyperthreading. This architecture is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and medium-scale database operations. It is less optimal for heavy cryptographic tasks or large-scale LLM inference due to the lack of hardware accelerators and lower encryption benchmark performance.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server utilizing the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and no hyperthreading. Operating at 2.6 GHz, it features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with an 8 Gbps baseline network speed. Local storage is not included. Benchmarks reveal top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidths in the top 25%, while overall CPU and database performance remains in the average tier. Cryptographic performance is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This server is highly efficient for multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and static web serving, but is less suited for heavy cryptographic workloads or large-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 32 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, ideal for multi-threaded processing and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server with 16 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 32 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core ARM64 compute instance featuring top-tier memory bandwidth for efficient multi-threaded processing and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton3 ARM64 physical cores at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-bandwidth system memory
Top-tier memory performance and strong large-block read/write bandwidth
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
